title: vicia angustifolia subsp. bobartii
intro: Vicia angustifolia subsp. bobartii, also known as the narrow-leaved vetch, is a plant species in the pea family (Fabaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Vicia angustifolia subsp. bobartii include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs and colds, and supporting respiratory health. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
